questions.text: The explanation of retrograde motion the Copernican Universe is...

answerChoices.text: planets are moving in circles inside of circles.
the earth changed direction, leading to an apparent change in the direction of a planet.
the direction of gravity reversed for that planet.
the rotation of the planet reversed.

questions.text: Kepler's First Law states that planets move...

answerChoices.text: in ellipses with the sun at one focus.
in ellipses with the sun at the center.
in a circle with the sun at the center.
in a circle with the earth at the center.

questions.text: Conceptually, what is the eccentricity of an ellipse?

answerChoices.text: A low eccentricity ellipse is close to a circle; a high eccentricity ellipse is close to an oval.
A low eccentricity ellipse is close to an oval; a high eccentricity ellipse is close to an circle.
A low eccentricity ellipse is concave; a high eccentricity ellipse is convex.
A low eccentricity ellipse is always curved; a high eccentricity ellipse has flat portions.

questions.text: What was Isaac Newton's contribution to the understanding of planets?

answerChoices.text: The orbit of planets derives from fundamental laws that also explain the motion of objects on earth.
Planets move in ellipses, not circles.
Planets orbit the sun, not the earth.
Planets were created by coalescing space dust.
